message:
fix(context): improve inline code detection for bash -c with single quotes

Fix detection of inline code in bash -c commands when using single quotes,
and add support for combined flags like -lc.

Problems fixed:
1. bash -c 'rm -rf /' was not marking the single-quoted content as InlineCode
   because the single-quote handler wasn't checking pending_inline_code state
2. bash -lc "cmd" was not detected because is_inline_code_flag() only checked
   for exact matches (-c, -e, -r, -S) not combined flags

Changes:
- Single quote handler now checks pending_inline_code and sets SpanKind::InlineCode
  when entering single-quoted content after a code flag
- Extract is_inline_code_flag() helper function that properly handles:
  - Exact match for -S (SSH ProxyCommand special case)
  - Combined short flags like -lc, -ec, -xc by checking if any character
    in the flag is c, e, or r (case-insensitive)
  - Excludes long flags (--something) to avoid false positives
- Fix closing delimiter handler to preserve current_kind instead of always
  using SpanKind::Data when exiting quoted regions

Tests added:
- test_bash_c_single_quote_inline_code: Verifies bash -c 'cmd' detection
- test_bash_lc_inline_code: Verifies bash -lc "cmd" detection

This improves heredoc and inline code pattern detection for shell commands
that execute code passed as string arguments.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
